A NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND AN EF0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN .6 MILES WNW OF THE INTERSECTION OF INDIAN VALLEY POST OFFICE ROAD AND VAUGHNS MILL ROAD...AND LIFTED ABOUT 1 MILE NORTHEAST NEAR SHADY GROVE ROAD.SEVERAL TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED AND A FEW SMALL STRUCTURES WERE DAMAGED.
A BARN WAS HEAVILY DAMAGED ON MANUEL ROAD. ONE TREE WAS DOWN ON A HOUSE ON LEDBETTER ROAD. NUMEROUS TREES DOWNED BETWEEN MANUEL ROAD AND JUST EAST OF JANET ROAD.

AN EF0 TORNADO WAS INTERMITTENTLY ON THE GROUND APPROXIMATELY 1.8 MILES FROM THE INTERSECTION OF PEAKS MOUNTAIN ROAD ALONG MARTHAS FORD ROAD EAST-NORTHEAST TO THE INTERSECTION WITH DICKEY ROAD. SEVERAL DOZEN TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED AND ONE STRUCTURE WAS SLIGHTLY DAMAGED BY A FALLING TREE. STORM TOUCHED DOWN IN GRAYSON COUNTY AND CROSSED INTO CARROLL COUNTY.

WATER...12 TO 18 INCHES DEEP...FLOWED ACROSS SCHOOL BOARD ROAD NEAR THE BOOKSIDE SWIM CLUB. THE ROAD WAS CLOSED AT 1215 AM EDT AND REOPENED AT 0300 AM EDT WHEN THE WATER RECEDED. THERE WAS NO DAMAGE AS THE WATER IMPACTED FIELDS ONLY.

*** 6 INJ *** AN EF2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON OSEE ROAD AND WAS ON THE GROUND FOR APPROXIMATELY 5 MILES MOVING EAST SOUTHEAST...FINALLY LIFTING BETWEEN OLD BARRETT ROAD AND GLADE VALLEY ROAD. A SINGLE WIDE MOBILE HOME WAS DESTROYED ON OSEE ROAD CAUSING 4 INJURIES. ALONG THE TRACK...CEMENT SILOS WERE COLLAPSED...5 OTHER HOMES WERE DAMAGED...AND SEVERAL STRUCTURES WERE HEAVILY DAMAGED. TWO OTHER PEOPLE RECEIVED MINOR INJURIED IN THEIR HOME WHEN THE TORNADO STRUCK.
AN EF1 TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED TOWN NEAR JARVIS ROAD. THIS TORANDO SNAPPED SEVERAL TREES AS IT TRACKED JUST A QUARTER MILE BEFORE LIFTING NEAR EARLY ROAD.

NUMEROUS MUD SLIDES WITH TREES DOWN BLOCKING OR PARTIALLY BLOCKING ROADS...HIGHWAY 18 SOUTH...HIGHWAY 268 WEST...AND SEVERAL COUNTY ROADS BETWEEN BOOMER AND THE W KERR SCOTT RESERVOIR.
